channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Lebanon's Shiite Muslims pay high price in war between Israel and Hezbollah
item.description: The Lebanese civilians most devastated by the Israel-Hezbollah war are Shiite Muslims, and many of them believe they are being unfairly punished because they share a religious identity with Hezbollah militants and often live in the same areas.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue And Kareem Chehayeb, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Namibia may elect its first-ever female president in elections this week
item.description: Namibia’s Vice President Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah could become the country’s first female president if she wins the presidential election Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Eliaser Ndeyanale, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An average of 140 women and girls were killed by a partner or relative per day in 2023, the UN says
item.description: Two U.N. agencies say an average of 140 women and girls were killed by an intimate partner or family member per day last year.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Uruguay’s leftist opposition candidate Yamandú Orsi becomes country's new president
item.description: Uruguay’s leftist opposition candidate, Yamandú Orsi, became the country's new president in a tight runoff election that ousted the conservative governing coalition.
item.dc:creator: Nayara Batschke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New Zealanders help to save about 30 whales after a pod strands on a beach
item.description: More than 30 pilot whales that stranded themselves on a beach in New Zealand have been safely returned to the ocean after conservation workers and residents helped to refloat them by lifting them on sheets.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Social media sites call for Australia to delay its ban on children younger than 16
item.description: An advocate for major social media platforms has told Australia's Parliament that a plan to ban children younger than 16 from the sites should be delayed rather than being rushed to approval this week.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korea holds memorial for forced laborers at Sado mines, a day after boycotting Japanese event
item.description: South Korea has paid tribute to wartime Korean forced laborers at Japan’s Sado Island Gold Mines in a memorial ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Left-wing opposition candidate wins tight presidential election in Uruguay
item.description: MONTEVIDEO, Uruguay (AP) — Uruguay's leftist opposition candidate, Yamandú Orsi, claimed victory in a tight presidential runoff Sunday, ousting the conservative governing coalition and making the South American nation the latest to rebuke the incumbent party in a year of landmark elections.
item.dc:creator: NAYARA BATSCHKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump team working 'hand in glove' with Biden administration on national security: Waltz
item.description: Rep. Michael Waltz (R-Fla.), President-elect Trump’s choice for national security adviser in his next administration, said Sunday that the Trump transition team is working “hand in glove” with the Biden administration on national security.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Israel says rabbi who went missing in the UAE was killed. The government arrests 3
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel said Sunday that the body of an Israeli-Moldovan rabbi who went missing in the United Arab Emirates has been found after he was killed in what it described as a “heinous antisemitic terror incident.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hezbollah fires barrage of rockets into Israel after IDF targets Hezbollah command centers in Beirut
item.description: Hezbollah on Sunday fired missiles and other projectiles into Israel in retaliation for to deadly IDF strikes on the militant group’s command centers in Beirut.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing Israeli rabbi who lived in UAE found dead, 3 arrested
item.description: A missing Israeli rabbi was found dead with three suspects being arrested in connection with the incident, according to a Jewish organization, United Arab Emirates (UAE) authorities and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: South African dissident writer and poet Breyten Breytenbach dies at 85
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — South African writer and poet Breyten Breytenbach, a staunch opponent of the former white-minority government’s apartheid policy of racial oppression, has died in Paris, his family announced Sunday. He was 85.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Four men convicted in Spain in connection to 2021 hate crime murder
item.description: Four men were sentenced for their involvement in the homophobic murder of 24-year-old Samuel Luiz, who died at a hospital in July 2021 after being attacked for allegedly being gay.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Intelligence Committee will ask Gabbard 'lots of questions,' Lankford says
item.description: Sen. James Lankford (R-Okla.) said Sunday that the Senate Intelligence Committee will ask President-elect Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, former Democratic Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), “lots of questions.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20 as diplomats push for a cease-fire
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes Saturday in central Beirut killed at least 20 people, officials said, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon's capital continued without warning while diplomats scrambled to broker a cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Second Australian teen dies in tainted alcohol case in Laos that has killed 6 tourists
item.description: VANG VIENG, Laos (AP) — A second Australian teenager who fell critically ill after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos has died in a hospital in Bangkok, her family said Friday, bringing the death toll in the mass poisoning of foreign tourists to six.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel keeping its ‘eyes open’ for Iranian attacks during Trump transition period, ambassador says
item.description: Israel's Ambassador to the United Nations Danny Danon speaks to Fox News Digital about the threat from Iran during the Trump transition period.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Death toll in Gaza from Israel-Hamas war passes 44,000, Palestinian officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The death toll in the Gaza Strip from the 13-month-old war between Israel and Hamas has surpassed 44,000, local health officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Top war-crimes court issues arrest warrants for Netanyahu and others in Israel-Hamas fighting
item.description: THE HAGUE (AP) — The world’s top war-crimes court issued arrest warrants Thursday for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, his former defense minister and Hamas’ military chief, accusing them of crimes against humanity in connection with the 13-month war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Brazil’s former President Bolsonaro and aides indicted for alleged 2022 coup attempt
item.description: Former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro and 36 others, including aides, were indicted by federal police on Thursday for allegedly attempting a coup to keep him in office in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US citizen among 4 dead in Laos after suspected alcohol poisoning
item.description: Four people have died after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos after officials said that multiple people had been sickened in town popular with backpackers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICC rejects Israeli appeals, issues arrest warrants for Benjamin Netanyahu, Yoav Gallant
item.description: The U.N.'s International Criminal Court approved arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and former Defense Minister Yoav Gallant.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine says Russia launched an intercontinental missile in an attack for the first time in the war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said a missile used “matches the speed and altitude” of an ICBM. The Kremlin refused to comment on the strike.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, WGN 9

item.title: Admiral: Middle East, Ukraine conflicts strain US air defense stocks
item.description: Conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine are adding a strain to the U.S. military’s ability to respond to China, a top U.S. admiral warns.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Biden's Ukraine missile move will have 'some impact': Retired major
item.description: Lyons questioned the timing of the decision, saying, “I’m not sure why we didn’t allow this two or three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: For the 2023-2024 school year, India saw a massive 23 percent increase in the number of college students sent to America.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Russia launches one of its fiercest missile and drone attacks at Ukraine's infrastructure
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian strike on a nine-story building in the city of Sumy in northern Ukraine killed eight people and wounded dozens, an official said Sunday, as Russia launched a massive drone and missile attack described by officials as the largest in recent months.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, NewsNation
